verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I TM p24-family trafficking protein predominantly resident in ER-Golgi cisternae, cycling within the early secretory pathway. NCBI explicitly notes plasma membrane localization in addition to Golgi, consistent with a minority surface pool delivered via secretory cycling. The TM topology places the GOLD/ectodomain on the extracellular face when at the PM. Dominant compartment is endomembrane, but a documented PM pool qualifies as contextual via dual localization.
